description: Single source of truth for the M5 inference engine's kernel-zoo coverage. Both `tools/quant_search/quant_driver.py` (which enumerates valid configs for the search grid) and the Metal engine's auto-loaders (which dispatch the right kernel for each loaded GGUF tensor) read this file. Adding a new (tensor_class, format) cell here is the trigger to add the corresponding kernel to kernels.swift; conversely, the engine validates at boot that every entry here resolves to a real switch case in its dispatchers, so this file and the kernel zoo can never silently drift.

Q8_0.notes: 8-bit (signed int8 + half scale). w = d * qs. Reference precision for dense weights.

Q6_K.notes: K-quant 6bpw with (4+2)-bit interleaved packing. w = dl * q - ml. Used for high-importance dense (attn_v, token_embd) in K-quant mixes.

Q5_K.notes: K-quant 5bpw with paired sub-block scales + 1-bit qh. w = dl * (lo + qh_val) - ml.

Q5_1.notes: Legacy 5bpw with per-block d, m, qh. w = d * (nib | qh_bit << 4) + m. Common for ffn_down_exps in older quants.

Q4_K.notes: K-quant 4bpw with paired sub-block scales. w = dl * nib - ml. Canonical for ffn_gate_up_exps in K-quant mixes.

Q4_1.notes: Legacy 4bpw with d + additive offset m. w = d * nib + m. Simpler than Q4_K, slightly higher bpw than Q4_0.

Q4_0.notes: Legacy 4bpw with d only. w = d * (nib - 8). Simplest 4bpw — minimal dequant ALU; fastest 4bpw on Apple Silicon.

F16.notes: IEEE half precision. Raw element-wise fp16; no block structure. Reference baseline.

token_embd.role: Embedding lookup table. Dequantized once at load to fp16 (no per-step kernel).

ffn_gate_up_exps.role: MoE expert gate+up weights, slot_token-broadcast convention (X[slot_token[s] * D_in]).

ffn_down_exps.role: MoE expert down-projection weights, per-slot convention (X[s * D_in]).

llama_quantize_mixes.note: Q5_0 is in llama-quantize but no kernel coverage on this engine; a Q5_0 GGUF would fail loading.
llama-quantize Q4_K_M canonical: shared dense Q8_0/Q5_K mix, MoE Q4_K up + Q5_1 down. The Q4_K_M GGUF you ship today.
llama-quantize Q5_K_M canonical: dense Q5_K (with Q6_K for attn_v on some layers, Q8_0 for ffn_down on some layers per llama-quantize's importance heuristics), MoE Q5_K up + Q8_0/Q5_1 down.
